pattern: Repeatedly combining the current first and last elements is just a two-pointer walk toward the middle, with a lone middle element handled as its own final step.
hints: You never actually need to delete elements from the array -- track a left and right pointer and walk them toward each other instead.
At each step, combine the digits of the value at the left pointer with the value at the right pointer (as if writing one number right after the other) and add that combined number to a running total.
When the pointers meet at the same index, that lone remaining value is added on its own, with no partner to combine it with.

edge_inputs.description: single element, added on its own with no partner
two elements combine into one concatenated value
a value with trailing zeros must not lose them when concatenated
the first-picked value has more digits than the last-picked value
maximum value repeated, exercising the largest possible concatenation
an odd-length array leaves exactly one middle element unpaired
single-digit values still concatenate as two-digit numbers, not summed

stress.description: random-length array of random four-digit-range values
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 1000))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 10000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
